name: Editorial Launch
description: A profile for expressive launch sites that need art direction, narrative pacing, and proof without becoming generic marketing.
licenseNotes: Extract editorial and brand-system principles. Do not copy agency case-study visuals, copy, or client identity assets.
principles: Use narrative sequence and visual contrast to create momentum.
Let imagery, typography, and product proof carry the page instead of decorative filler.
Prefer a point of view over neutral SaaS sameness.
positiveExamples: A first viewport with a clear product artifact and memorable editorial posture.
A section sequence that changes tempo without losing usability.
antiPatterns: Awwwards-style visuals with no product explanation.
Massive typography that hides weak substance.
typographyRules: Pair display-scale headings with concise supporting copy.
Use section labels sparingly and consistently.
layoutRules: Use asymmetry, contrast, and whitespace, but preserve mobile reading order.
Every expressive section needs a product or brand reason.
componentRules: Buttons and nav must remain simple even when the art direction is expressive.
copyRules: Avoid luxury vagueness; every poetic line must be backed by a concrete detail.
screenshotNotes: Capture wide desktop and mobile to verify the art direction survives responsive collapse.
